20a.question: Which square appears darker, A or B? Are they the same shade of gray?

21a.description: Two halves separated by opposing gradients at center boundary. Both halves are identical gray (140,140,140) except for 30px gradient zone at center.
21a.actual: identical (both RGB 140,140,140) except at center boundary
21a.question: Do the left and right halves of 21a appear to be the same shade, or does one look lighter?

24a.A_context: replacing part of light stripe (flanked by dark stripes above/below)
24a.B_context: replacing part of dark stripe (flanked by light stripes above/below)
24a.human_perception: A appears darker than B despite identical RGB
24a.question: Do patches A and B appear the same shade? Which looks lighter?

29a.description: 6 uniform bands of increasing brightness (40 to 215). Each band is internally uniform. Humans see illusory dark/light edges at boundaries (Mach bands effect).
29a.question: Are the bands internally uniform, or do you see any variation within each band (especially at the edges between bands)?

30a.description: 5x5 grid of dark squares with light gray gaps. Humans typically see faint dark spots at the intersections of the light gaps (where they're not looking directly).
30a.question: Do you see any phantom dots or spots at the intersections of the gaps between the squares?

30b.description: Inverted: light squares on dark background. Humans may see bright phantom spots at dark intersections.
30b.question: Do you see phantom spots in 30b?
